name: ziggurat
description: A modern, lightweight HTTP server framework for Zig that prioritizes performance, safety, and developer experience.

releases.name: v0.2.0.0
releases.body:
v2.0.0

  Breaking Changes

  - initGlobalCorsConfig now requires a CorsConfig parameter. Previously it accepted only an allocator and created a default config. Update calls from initGlobalCorsConfig(allocator) to
  initGlobalCorsConfig(allocator, .{}), or pass specific values. If using features.initialize, no changes needed — it passes the config through automatically.
  - middleware_fn is deprecated. Use useMiddleware instead (same signature). The examples have been updated.

  New Features

  - Thread pool. The server now uses a fixed pool of worker threads (default 32) instead of spawning a new thread per connection. Configurable via .threadPoolSize() on the builder.
  - Connection backpressure. Incoming connections queue up to a configurable limit (default 512). When full, new connections are rejected with 503 — no allocation required. Configurable via
  .maxConnections().
  - HTTP/1.1 keep-alive. Connections are reused for multiple requests (default: up to 100 requests or 15s idle). The server parses the Connection header and responds with Connection: keep-alive or
  Connection: close. Arena allocators reset between requests in O(1). Configurable via .keepAliveTimeout() and .maxRequestsPerConnection().
  - Graceful shutdown. server.stop() stops accepting new connections and drains in-flight requests for up to 30s (configurable via .drainTimeout()). Workers finish their current request but don't accept new
   ones on keep-alive connections. start() returns after all workers have joined.
  - Version constant. ziggurat.version is now available at comptime, sourced from build.zig.zon via build options. Only one place to update.
  - Windows socket timeout support. Timeout handling now works correctly on Windows (uses u32 milliseconds instead of POSIX timeval).

  Bug Fixes

  - CORS memory leak. corsMiddleware allocated 5 header strings with page_allocator on every OPTIONS request and never freed them. Headers are now pre-built once at init time. The middleware path allocates
  nothing.
  - Double-close on listener socket. Calling stop() then deinit() closed the listener fd twice (undefined behavior). Fixed with a sentinel guard.
  - ErrorHandlerConfig memory leak. initGlobalErrorHandler heap-allocated a config struct but deinitGlobalErrorHandler never freed it.
  - Logger never deinitialized. features.deinitialize() now calls deinitGlobalLogger() with correct teardown order (reverse of init).
  - Rate limiter returned wrong status. rateLimitMiddleware returned 408 (Request Timeout) instead of 429 (Too Many Requests).
  - setUserData didn't handle sentinel-terminated slices. Values from @tagName() (which returns [:0]const u8) now work correctly.
  - Examples didn't compile. Calls to server.middleware(...) (which doesn't exist) changed to server.useMiddleware(...).
  - Test handlers used wrong status codes. forbiddenHandler returned 400 instead of 403, authMiddleware returned 400 instead of 401, methodPostHandler returned 200 instead of 201, methodDeleteHandler
  returned 200 instead of 204.

  Cleanup

  - Removed dead metricsMiddleware function (duplicated startRequestMetrics).
  - Removed duplicate logging convenience functions from logging.zig (canonical versions are in log.zig).
  - Removed all ticket/PR references from source comments (#9, #10, #13, #21, #22).
  - Removed ~220 lines of narrating comments from test files.
  - Simplified session middleware from nested 3-branch structure to flat flow.
  - Extracted setSockTimeout helper from duplicated timeout construction code.
  - Removed duplicate origin_header global from CORS module (reuses preflight_strs[0]).
  - Docs updated to reflect thread pool model, keep-alive, graceful shutdown, and git+ fetch URL.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ooyeku/ziggurat/compare/v1.0.0...v2.0.0
